text.title: Retention Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Retention Strategies` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pick ONE primary retention loop matching product's natural use; optionally ONE secondary. Stacking 3+ loops simultaneously splits attention + dilutes mechanic clarity.

rationale.source: Reforge retention loops curriculum; product casework

rule.rationale: Each loop consumes UX surface + cognitive load. 3+ loops = none feels primary; users disengage from all. One sharp loop > four mediocre ones.

rule.statement: Loop archetype MUST match product's natural use (content product → content loop; social product → network loop). Forcing wrong-shape loop is the #1 retention mistake.
rule.rationale: Forcing a viral loop on a single-player productivity tool produces nothing. Match: content app → publishing loop; social → friend-graph loop; workflow → habit/streak; marketplace → buyer-seller loop.

rule.statement: Engagement mechanics (streaks, achievements, variable rewards) MUST run as a 4-6 week A/B test on a cohort before global rollout. Untested mechanics often reduce retention by triggering quit-impulses.

rationale.source: Nir Eyal hook model; A/B test postmortems

rule.rationale: Mechanics that look good in mockups can backfire — streak break = user quits forever. A/B test catches the inversion before it touches whole base.

rule.statement: Re-engagement campaigns MUST run within 30-90 days of last active. Beyond 90 days, conversion drops > 70%.
rule.rationale: Recency matters. After 90 days the user is a cold lead, not a re-engagement target. Resources better spent on net-new acquisition.

rule.statement: When using reward mechanics, MUST use variable-ratio schedule (random reward size / timing) not fixed. Fixed rewards extinct after habit forms.

rationale.source: Skinner variable-ratio research; Duolingo retention case study

rule.rationale: Fixed rewards become predictable + ignored after habituation. Variable schedule sustains engagement long-term — same psychological mechanism as slot machines (with the ethical hedge).

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
